# Canon Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

Canon is an open source project for governed, AI-assisted engineering work. We
want repository participation to stay respectful, constructive, and safe for
contributors, maintainers, and users across issues, pull requests, reviews,
release discussions, and documentation changes.

By participating in this project, you agree to help keep the community
professional, inclusive, and focused on the work.

## Expected Behavior

Examples of behavior that support a healthy project include:

- being respectful in technical disagreement
- giving clear, specific, and constructive feedback
- assuming good intent until evidence shows otherwise
- keeping critique focused on code, docs, behavior, and decisions rather than on people
- taking responsibility for mistakes and correcting them quickly
- keeping security, privacy, and responsible disclosure concerns in mind

## Unacceptable Behavior

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

- harassment, intimidation, stalking, or threats
- hateful, discriminatory, or demeaning language or conduct
- sexualized language, imagery, or unwanted sexual attention
- publishing private or identifying information without explicit permission
- personal attacks, dogpiling, or sustained hostile behavior
- spam, deliberate disruption, or repeated off-topic escalation
- knowingly misrepresenting evidence, validation, authorship, or review results

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies in spaces under project control, including:

- GitHub issues, pull requests, comments, and code review
- repository documentation, examples, and contributed artifacts
- release, packaging, and workflow discussions connected to the project
- any other public or private project space where someone is representing Canon

## Reporting

If you experience or witness behavior that violates this Code of Conduct, report
it to the project maintainers through a private GitHub contact path when one is
available.

If no private channel is visible, open a minimal issue requesting a private
maintainer contact path and do not include sensitive personal details,
harassment details, or exploit information in public.

If the report is also a security concern, follow [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md)
for the vulnerability reporting path.

## Enforcement

Maintainers may take any action they judge necessary to protect the project and
participants, including:

- requesting a change in behavior
- moderating, editing, locking, or removing content
- closing issues or pull requests
- rejecting contributions
- temporarily restricting participation
- permanently banning a participant from project spaces

Maintainers will try to review reports fairly, proportionally, and with care for
the privacy and safety of the people involved.

## Attribution

This document is informed by the structure of the Contributor Covenant Code of
Conduct, adapted to this repository's current communication surfaces.
